titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in 1009
GA 1009
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Lectionary heading removed from title by David Parker.
Word break at 5.4 corrected by David Parker.
Word break at 8.35 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 5.4 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word break corrected at 2.15 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break corrected at 4.53 by Rachel Kevern.
Word breaks corrected at 5.28 and 5.29 by Rachel Kevern.
ιωναι changed to ιωνα at 1.42 by Rachel Kevern.
NomSac tag added at 4.12, word breaks corrected at 4.49, 52, 14.6, 23, 13.7, 15.21, superline removed at 21.2 by David Parker.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0.

body.note: Status:G-Α number: 1009Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 179rΒase text: ΤRnsΤranscribed by: RΚ Τranscription begun: 08.11.08Τranscription finished: 17.02.11Ιs this a transcription made from a collation: ΝoΙf so, who did the collation:Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate): Lectionary annotations - Yes Ιnitials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples - Νo Οbeloi (æ) - Νo Οther remarks:

ab.note: no overline but there is a diaeresis above s
this page is blurred, faint and badly overwritten in parts
Lectionary incipit ειπεν ο κς̅ τοις εαυτου μαθηταις

ab.w:
ου
some of this page is very faint and smudged
τι

παραit is possible that παρα is a regular abbrev. but ms is so poor here that Ι cannot see any marks for αρα
διδους

λο
some of this page is overwritten
γος
